message:
fix(daemon): short-poll idle reads so shutdown doesn't stall behind request_timeout [coding_agent_session_search-a5z57]

Before: handle_connection set the stream's read_timeout to the
configured request_timeout (60s default) and called read_exact on
the length prefix. An idle client that opened the socket and never
sent bytes pinned the handler inside that single blocking read.
Because the connection handlers run inside std::thread::scope,
ModelDaemon::run could not finish shutdown until every such handler
either received a request, timed out, or disconnected — so a
single idle peer turned SIGTERM into a ~60 second hang.

After: idle reads short-poll on a bounded IDLE_SHUTDOWN_POLL
interval (250ms, clamped to request_timeout). Between polls the
handler checks self.shutdown and returns Ok(()) immediately when
set. Configured request_timeout semantics are preserved: the manual
retry loop tracks total elapsed and bails at the original budget,
and partial bytes are preserved across retries (read_exact would
discard them). Once bytes are in flight the payload read reverts to
the full request_timeout so we don't kick a slow sender mid-frame.

Regression test: handle_connection_returns_promptly_when_shutdown_set_during_idle_read
spawns a handler bound to one end of a UnixStream::pair with a 10s
request_timeout, lets it settle into the idle read, sets the
shutdown flag, and asserts shutdown latency < 2s (regression would
sit near 10s). All 8 daemon::core::tests pass in 0.28s total.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
